mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which forces equity valuations lower over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and stable consumer prices indicates robust demand without inflationary pressure, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3 percent annualized growth alongside RSAFS expansion in 70 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening economic output and capacity utilization, which translates into immediate positive market sentiment and S&P 500 gains over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 70 percent of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates with negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of monthly observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts combined with slowing GDP growth signals a broad demand shock that reduces construction sector earnings and drags down S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined housing and GDP decline fails to predict negative returns when FEDFUNDS drops by greater than 0.5 percent concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production breaking above the 110.0 threshold indicates peak capacity utilization and strong manufacturing demand, triggering immediate positive S&P 500 momentum over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Returns turn negative in greater than 55 percent of periods when INDPRO crosses the 110.0 threshold over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates alongside accelerating inflation compress profit margins and increase discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days during tightening cycles.
failure_condition: The dual signal of rising rates and inflation predicts positive returns in greater than 60 percent of observations during tightening phases.
